message:
perf(mvcc): O(1) atomic occupancy counter for lock table

The lock table was performing O(capacity)=O(1M) full-table scans on
every lock acquisition to check the load factor. This was a critical
hot-path bottleneck that dominated SSI validation overhead.

Solution: Add atomic occupancy counter (AtomicU32) that tracks occupied
slots and is incremented atomically after successful CAS during slot
claiming. The counter provides an approximate count with bounded slack
from concurrent in-flight operations (typically < 100), which is
negligible for the 70% load factor threshold check.

Complexity reduction: O(1M) → O(1) per lock acquisition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
